content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 19, 1990

Wes Van Winkle
Bagatelos & Fadem
601 California Street, Suite 1801
San Francisco, California 94108

Re: Your Request For Confirmation
Of Telephone Assistance
Our File No. A-90-578

Dear Mr. Van Winkle:

You have requested confirmation of telephone assistance
provided to you on behalf of your client "Yes on 126 - supporting
the Alcohol Abuse and Drug Education Act of 1990, sponsored by
Beer, Wine and Distilled Spirits Companies," under the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

Your question concerns Regulation 18225(b)(4)(c) which
provides an exception from the definition of expenditure for
communications sent by an organization to its members, employees,
shareholders, other affiliated individuals and those who request
or purchase the publication.

In accordance with the judgment in Service Employees
International Union, AFL-CIO, CLC, et al. v. Fair Political
Practices Commission, Case No. CIVS 89-0433 LKK (dated September
25, 1990; modified by the court order of September 28, 1990), we
revise the advice provided to you by telephone as follows:

Costs in connection with a political communication sent by an
organization as part of its regularly published newsletter which
complies with the criteria set out in Regulation 18225(b)(4)(c)
are not reportable expenditures or contributions, even if the
communication is made at the behest of a candidate or committee.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Wes Van Winkle
Page Two


     Enclosed for your information is the Lowell Advice Letter
(No. A-90-581), which addresses the issue of the applicability of
the exception for regularly published newsletters to
communications which are at the behest of a candidate or
committee.

     We provide the following advice which was not discussed in
the telephone conversation:

     In the facts presented in your letter, the committee would
request that certain membership organizations include an
endorsement of the ballot measure for which the committee is
primarily formed in the associations' regularly published
newsletters.2/  The newsletters would be distributed to "customers
of the association members who have either requested that they be
placed on the newsletters mailing list, or have been placed on the
mailing list because they have become regular customers of the
association member."

     Regulation 18225(b)(4) provides:

     Notwithstanding the provisions of this subsection,
the term expenditure does not include costs incurred
for communications which expressly advocate the nomination,
election or defeat of a clearly identified candidate or
candidates or the qualification, passage or defeat of a
clearly identified measure or measures by:

          (A) A regularly published newspaper, magazine or
other periodical of general circulation which
routinely carries news, articles and commentary
of general interest.

          (B) A federally regulated broadcast outlet.

          (C) A regularly published newsletter or
regularly published periodical, other than those
specified in paragraph (b)(4)(A), whose circulation
is limited to an organization's members, employees,
shareholders, other affiliated individuals and
those who request or purchase the publication.
This paragraph applies only to the costs regularly
incurred in publishing and distributing the
newsletter or periodical. If additional costs are

2/  This advice does not address the question of whether the
newsletters to which you refer are "regularly published
newsletters."

Wes Van Winkle
Page Three


                    incurred because the newsletter or periodical is
                    issued on other than its regular schedule, expanded
                    in circulation, or substantially altered in style,
                    size or format, the additional costs are
                    expenditures.

                                        (Emphasis added.)

     This exception does not apply to a person's customers who
have not requested that the newsletter be sent to them. Therefore,
all costs attributable to producing and sending the communication
to customers who have not requested the newsletter, or to any
other persons who are not included within the scope of the
exception in Regulation 18225(b)(4)(C) are reportable expenditures
or contributions

     If you have any questions about this letter, please call
me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel
                                        [signature]
                                        By: Jeanne Pritchard
                                            Chief, Technical Assistance and
                                            Analysis Division

Encl.
